sourcesContent:
import React from 'react'; import { Link } from 'react-router-dom'; const mockPhotos = { food: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1498837167922-ddd27525d352?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', appliance: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1484154218962-a197022b5858?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', campus: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1523050854058-8df90110c9f1?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', }; function Home({ currentUser }) { return (
Campus reuse platform

CampusCycle makes local reuse feel simple, credible, and immediate.

A refined marketplace for students to circulate appliances, dorm essentials, and everyday goods before they turn into avoidable waste.

Browse listings {currentUser ? ( View your profile ) : ( Create account )}
CampusCycle
Reuse in action Mini fridge

Collected in Harvard Square and kept in use instead of discarded at move-out.

School Harvard
Region Greater Boston
Purpose Reduce food waste. Extend appliance life. Make student reuse visible.
Why it matters

A professional marketplace informed by environmental education.

When students move quickly, usable fridges, cookware, storage, and unopened food are often left behind. CampusCycle reframes that transition as an opportunity for reuse, affordability, and better environmental decision-making.

Food systems

Usable food should support students, not become avoidable waste.

A significant share of move-out waste comes from unopened or lightly used groceries. Pantry exchanges, shared meal supplies, and coordinated pickup can turn disposal into access.

Appliance reuse

Durable appliances should stay in circulation longer.

Small appliances are often abandoned because resale feels inconvenient. A more polished marketplace changes that behavior by making local reuse faster, clearer, and more trustworthy.

Food waste

Surplus groceries can become shared value.

End-of-semester transitions often leave students with unopened pantry items, meal ingredients, and kitchen basics they cannot carry home. A local exchange model helps keep those resources in active use.

Appliance waste

Useful appliances should not be treated as disposable.

Mini fridges, lamps, fans, kettles, and microwaves are costly to replace and inefficient to discard. Extending their life, even briefly, can reduce both waste and student expense.

Educational dashboard

Give each listing a clearer sustainability context.

Environmental messaging is more effective when it is specific. These mock visuals translate everyday campus behavior into simple signals that make reuse feel practical, local, and measurable.

Mock weekly diversion Items redirected from waste
Food
72%
Appliances
61%
Storage
84%
Mock semester trend Participation improves when reuse is visible

Mock data: when reuse activity is easy to see, educational messaging becomes more actionable.

Educational focus

Support better habits at the exact moment students need them.

The most effective sustainability tools are practical. Instead of relying on abstract messaging, CampusCycle makes reuse visible through nearby listings, regional pickup, trust cues, and a clearer handoff experience.

Reuse first Guide student decisions toward keeping useful goods in circulation.
Regional pickup School, region, and area filters make local reuse feel immediate.
Visible impact Each listing can communicate the practical value of avoiding unnecessary disposal.
Design rationale

A calmer visual system builds more trust.

The green-led palette signals environmental purpose and trust. Soft neutrals keep the interface premium. Generous white space reduces friction. Together, those decisions make the platform feel more like a student service and less like an informal classifieds board.

Green-led action Primary actions communicate growth, care, and environmental responsibility.
Calm neutral surfaces Soft backgrounds create a more stable reading experience in educational sections.
Data with context Charts, photography, and concise copy help make the mission feel tangible.
Start locally

Built for campus communities that want lower waste and better handoffs.

Browse listings View mock checkout
); } export default Home;
